turns.content: how does the sun shine
how does the sun shine
The Sun shines because deep in its core, tiny bits of hydrogen squash together and release light and heat. What do you think it would feel like to stand near something that hot?
super super hot! but how do the bits squash together
why does the moon change shape
why is it day and night
It is because the Earth spins around like a slow top, so one side faces the Sun while the other is in shadow. If you were standing on the Earth, would you feel it spinning?
no i dont feel anything! so how do we know it spins
what is a star made of
why do stars twinkle but planets do not
how does the heart pump blood
why does my heart go thump thump
That thump is your heart squeezing to push blood all around your body, a bit like a hand squeezing a water balloon. Where do you think the blood goes after it leaves your heart?
maybe to my arms and legs? does it come back again
why do we breathe
what does the brain do
how does food get digested
Your body breaks food into tiny pieces, starting in your mouth and then in your tummy, so it can soak up the good parts. What do you think your teeth are doing to start that job?
chewing it up small! then what happens in my tummy
what makes my tummy growl when I am hungry
how do magnets work
what is electricity
Electricity is the flow of tiny particles called electrons moving through wires, a bit like water flowing through a pipe. What kinds of things in your home need electricity to work?
the lights and the tv! how does it get into the wires
how does a battery store energy
how does a lever work
what makes sound
how does a mirror work
A mirror has a smooth shiny surface that bounces light straight back to your eyes, so you see a reflection. What happens if the surface is bumpy instead of smooth?
maybe the picture gets all wobbly? why does smooth make it clear
how do plants make food
how do seeds sprout germinate
A seed wakes up when it gets water and warmth, then pushes out a tiny root and shoot. What do you think the little root is looking for under the soil?
water? and maybe food in the dirt
what is a food chain
what are cells
how do birds fly
what happened to the dinosaurs
Many scientists think a huge space rock hit the Earth and changed the weather so much that the dinosaurs could not survive. What do you think happened to the plants the dinosaurs ate?
maybe they died too? so the dinosaurs had no food
what makes rain fall from clouds
how do snowflakes form six arms
Snowflakes grow as water freezes into ice crystals, and the way water molecules lock together makes six sides. Have you ever noticed if any two snowflakes look exactly the same?
no they all look different! why is that
what causes wind to blow
why does lightning make thunder
how do volcanoes erupt magma
why is the sky blue and not green
Sunlight is made of many colours, and the air scatters the blue light around more than the others, so the sky looks blue. What colour does the sky turn when the Sun is setting?
orange and pink! why does it change then
